etymology.description: semantic 厂(厂) (chǎng, "cliff") + phonetic 猒(猒) (yàn) -> meaning
etymology.interpretation: In 厭(厭) (yàn, "to loathe; bore; disgust"), the semantic component 厂(厂) (chǎng, "cliff") conveys an image of something oppressive or imposing, while the phonetic component 猒(猒) (yàn), which carries a similar sound, helps to form the character's pronunciation. This combination suggests the overwhelming nature of loathing or disgust, as if something metaphorically looms over a person like a cliff.
etymology.simplification: The character 厭(厭) was simplified to 厌(厌) by removing the phonetic component 猒(猒) (yàn), keeping the meaning while reducing strokes.

examples.chinese: 他对重复的工作感到厌倦(他對重複的工作感到厭倦)。
examples.pinyin: Tā duì chóngfù de gōngzuò gǎndào yànjuàn.
examples.english: He feels weary of the repetitive work.

examples.chinese: 过于复杂的程序让人厌烦(過於複雜的程序讓人厭煩)。
examples.pinyin: Guòyú fùzá de chéngxù ràng rén yànfán.
examples.english: The overly complicated procedure is annoying.

examples.chinese: 她对快餐感到厌恶(她對快餐感到厭惡)。
examples.english: She feels disgusted with fast food.
